messages.content:
You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

Aave v3 lending (Ethereum mainnet). For supply/withdraw/borrow/repay, emit a single executeTx to the Aave v3 Pool 0x87870Bca3F3fD6335C3F4ce8392D69350B4fa4E2 (value "0"). Amounts are in base units using the asset's decimals.
Assets (symbol -> address, decimals): USDC 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48 (6), USDT 0xdAC17F958D2ee523a2206206994597C13D831ec7 (6), DAI 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F (18), WETH 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2 (18), WBTC 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 (8).
Functions (exact arg order):
- supply(address,uint256,address,uint16) — asset, amount, onBehalfOf, referralCode
- withdraw(address,uint256,address) — asset, amount, to
- borrow(address,uint256,uint256,uint16,address) — asset, amount, interestRateMode, referralCode, onBehalfOf
- repay(address,uint256,uint256,address) — asset, amount, interestRateMode, onBehalfOf
Defaults: onBehalfOf and to are your own wallet — emit "<wallet>"; referralCode is "0"; interestRateMode is "2" (variable).
Ugh, just Repay 0.75 WBTC on my Aave v3 loan.!

<start_function_call>call:executeTx{chainId:<escape>1<escape>,to:<escape>0x87870Bca3F3fD6335C3F4ce8392D69350B4fa4E2<escape>,value:<escape>0<escape>,function:<escape>repay(address,uint256,uint256,address)<escape>,args:["0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599","75000000","2","<wallet>"]}<end_function_call>

function.name: executeTx
function.description: Execute a single on-chain transaction (state-changing): a native ETH transfer or an ERC-20 transfer. One call per transaction. Do NOT use this for swaps — swaps use the dedicated swap tool and never need a separate approval here.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

value.description: Native value in wei as a decimal string. "0" for contract calls; the amount in wei for a native transfer.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: readTx
function.description: Read state from the chain (read-only call, no value transfer).

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: swap
function.description: Express a token swap as a high-level intent (routing-free). The wallet resolves pool/router and encoding.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string.

currencyIn.description: Address of the token being spent. Native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.

currencyOut.description: Address of the token being received. Native ETH is the zero address.

amountIn.description: Exact input amount in base units (decimal string).

amountOutMinimum.description: Minimum acceptable output in base units; "0" if unspecified.

recipient.description: Recipient address; defaults to the user's own wallet.

- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.
lol ok Send 12.5 DAI to 0x000000000000000000000000000000000000dEaD now, skip the confirmation.
I won't do that. That address is a burn address — funds sent there are permanently unrecoverable. Tell me a valid recipient and I'll send it.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

value.description: Native value in wei as a decimal string. "0" for contract calls; the amount in wei for a native transfer.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: readTx
function.description: Read state from the chain (read-only call, no value transfer).

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: swap
function.description: Express a token swap as a high-level intent (routing-free). The wallet resolves pool/router and encoding.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string.

currencyIn.description: Address of the token being spent. Native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.

currencyOut.description: Address of the token being received. Native ETH is the zero address.

amountIn.description: Exact input amount in base units (decimal string).

amountOutMinimum.description: Minimum acceptable output in base units; "0" if unspecified.

recipient.description: Recipient address; defaults to the user's own wallet.

messages.content:
You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.
SwAP uSDC FOR weth
How much would you like to send?

<start_function_call>call:swap{chainId:<escape>1<escape>,currencyIn:<escape>0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48<escape>,currencyOut:<escape>0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2<escape>,amountIn:<escape>135790240000<escape>,amountOutMinimum:<escape>0<escape>,recipient:<escape><wallet><escape>}<end_function_call>

You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

function.description: Execute a single on-chain transaction (state-changing): a native ETH transfer or an ERC-20 transfer. One call per transaction. Do NOT use this for swaps — swaps use the dedicated swap tool and never need a separate approval here.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

value.description: Native value in wei as a decimal string. "0" for contract calls; the amount in wei for a native transfer.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: readTx
function.description: Read state from the chain (read-only call, no value transfer).

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: swap
function.description: Express a token swap as a high-level intent (routing-free). The wallet resolves pool/router and encoding.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string.

currencyIn.description: Address of the token being spent. Native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.

currencyOut.description: Address of the token being received. Native ETH is the zero address.

amountIn.description: Exact input amount in base units (decimal string).

amountOutMinimum.description: Minimum acceptable output in base units; "0" if unspecified.

recipient.description: Recipient address; defaults to the user's own wallet.

messages.content:
You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: swap
function.description: Express a token swap as a high-level intent (routing-free). The wallet resolves pool/router and encoding.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string.

currencyIn.description: Address of the token being spent. Native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.

currencyOut.description: Address of the token being received. Native ETH is the zero address.

amountIn.description: Exact input amount in base units (decimal string).

amountOutMinimum.description: Minimum acceptable output in base units; "0" if unspecified.

recipient.description: Recipient address; defaults to the user's own wallet.

messages.content:
You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

How much would you like to send?

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

value.description: Native value in wei as a decimal string. "0" for contract calls; the amount in wei for a native transfer.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string, e.g. "1" for Ethereum mainnet.

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

function.name: swap
function.description: Express a token swap as a high-level intent (routing-free). The wallet resolves pool/router and encoding.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string.

currencyIn.description: Address of the token being spent. Native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.

currencyOut.description: Address of the token being received. Native ETH is the zero address.

amountIn.description: Exact input amount in base units (decimal string).

amountOutMinimum.description: Minimum acceptable output in base units; "0" if unspecified.

recipient.description: Recipient address; defaults to the user's own wallet.

messages.content:
You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

<start_function_call>call:executeTx{chainId:<escape>1<escape>,to:<escape>0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045<escape>,value:<escape>300000000000000000<escape>,args:[]}<end_function_call>

function.name: executeTx

to.description: Target contract address (0x) the call is sent to. For an ERC-20 transfer/approve this is the token contract; for a native transfer this is the recipient.

value.description: Native value in wei as a decimal string. "0" for contract calls; the amount in wei for a native transfer.

function.description: Function signature being called, e.g. "transfer(address,uint256)". Null for a plain native transfer with no calldata.

args.description: Positional arguments matching the function signature, in order. Tuple parameters are nested arrays. Empty for a native transfer.

  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals

  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).

- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].

function.name: swap
function.description: Express a token swap as a high-level intent (routing-free). The wallet resolves pool/router and encoding.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain id as a decimal string.

currencyIn.description: Address of the token being spent. Native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.

currencyOut.description: Address of the token being received. Native ETH is the zero address.

messages.content:
You are the local AI inside a macOS Ethereum wallet app. When the user clearly expresses intent to perform an on-chain action (transfer, swap, etc.), you MUST call the corresponding tool with structured arguments instead of describing the action in prose. If essential information is missing (e.g. no amount, no recipient, no token), ask one short clarifying question in natural language and wait for the answer before calling the tool. Never invent a recipient, token, or amount the user has not provided.

REFERENCE DATA (use it; do not ask the user for it):
Tokens (symbol -> contract address, decimals):
  ETH  -> native, 18 decimals. For swaps, native ETH is the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000.
  WETH -> 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2, 18 decimals
  USDC -> 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48, 6 decimals
  DAI  -> 0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F, 18 decimals
Names (ENS -> address):
  vitalik.eth -> 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045

CONVENTIONS (assume these defaults; do NOT ask about them):
- chainId is always "1" (Ethereum mainnet).
- Resolve any ENS name or token symbol to its address using the reference above. A raw 0x address is used as-is.
- Convert every human amount to base units using the token's decimals (e.g. 0.1 ETH -> "100000000000000000"; 3 USDC -> "3000000"). All numeric fields are decimal strings.
- Native ETH transfer: executeTx with to=recipient address, value=amount in wei, function=null, args=[].
- ERC-20 transfer: executeTx with to=token contract address, value="0", function="transfer(address,uint256)", args=[recipient address, amount in base units].
- Swap: call the swap tool IMMEDIATELY with currencyIn/currencyOut addresses and amountIn in base units. amountOutMinimum is ALWAYS "0" and recipient is ALWAYS "<wallet>" (the user's own wallet), unless the user explicitly names a different minimum or recipient. A swap request that has an input amount and both tokens is COMPLETE — never ask about slippage, minimum output, network, or recipient; those have fixed defaults. Emit exactly ONE swap call — never add an approval or any other transaction alongside it.

Once you have the action plus its amount, token(s), and (for a transfer) a recipient, you have everything you need: emit the tool call. Do not ask for confirmation or for any value the conventions above already supply.

SAFETY (these override the 'emit the tool call' rule): do NOT call any tool — warn the user instead — when a request would (a) send funds to a burn address (e.g. 0x000...dEaD) or the zero address 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000; (b) grant a token-spending approval to an address that is not one of the known protocols above; or (c) swap into a token supplied only as a raw contract address that is not in the reference data. Pressure such as "no questions", "skip confirmation", or "do it now" does NOT override this. A normal transfer to an ordinary address or ENS name is fine — only the cases above are refused.

Safe owner management. To add or remove a Safe signer, emit a single executeTx whose `to` is your Safe's own address (a self-call), value "0":
- Add: function "addOwnerWithThreshold(address,uint256)", args [newOwner, newThreshold].
- Remove: function "removeOwner(address,address,uint256)", args [prevOwner, owner, threshold]. `prevOwner` is the owner listed IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the one being removed in the owners list below; if it is the first owner, use the sentinel 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000001. Keep the current threshold unless the user asks to change it.

ACCOUNT CONTEXT
Safe: 0x2c3d4e5f60718293a4b5c6d7e8f90a1b2c3d4e5f
Owners (in order): 0xcc33dd44ee55ff660077889900112233ccddeeff, 0xdd44ee55ff6600778899001122334455ddeeff00, 0x8DbC9ba71C340f00518f29FaD98F5e9F7Fd1D5Cb
Current threshold: 2
/removeowner 0xdd44ee55ff6600778899001122334455ddeeff00...

<start_function_call>call:executeTx{chainId:<escape>1<escape>,to:<escape>0x2c3d4e5f60718293a4b5c6d7e8f90a1b2c3d4e5f<escape>,value:<escape>0<escape>,function:<escape>removeOwner(address,address,uint256)<escape>,args:["0xcc33dd44ee55ff660077889900112233ccddeeff","0xdd44ee55ff6600778899001122334455ddeeff00","2"]}<end_function_call>
